CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path.name: CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path
CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path.brief: Computes shortest surface paths from one or more source points on a surface mesh.
CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path.detailed: Uses an optimized variation of Chen and Han's $O(n^2)$ algorithm by Xin and Wang. Refer to those respective papers for the details of the implementation. Traits a model of SurfaceMeshShortestPathTraits . VIM a model of ReadablePropertyMap with vertex_descriptor as key and unsigned int as value type. The default is boost::property_map<HG, boost::vertex_index_t>::const_type . HIM a model of ReadablePropertyMap with halfedge_descriptor as key and unsigned int as value type. The default is boost::property_map<HG, boost::halfedge_index_t>::const_type . FIM a model of ReadablePropertyMap with face_descriptor as key and unsigned int as value type. The default is boost::property_map<HG, boost::face_index_t>::const_type . VPM a model of ReadablePropertyMap with vertex_descriptor as key and Traits::Point_3 as value type. The default is boost::property_map<HG, CGAL::vertex_point_t>::const_type . If index property maps are not provided through the constructor of the class, internal property maps must be available and initialized. CGAL::set_halfedgeds_items_id()

members.name: Barycentric_coordinates
members.brief: An ordered triple which specifies a location inside a triangle as a convex combination of its three vertices.
members.definition: typedef Traits::Barycentric_coordinates CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::Barycentric_coordinates

members.name: Barycentric_coordinate
members.detailed: Deprecated
members.definition: typedef Barycentric_coordinates CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::Barycentric_coordinate

members.name: Face_location
members.brief: An ordered pair specifying a location on the surface of the Triangle_mesh .
members.detailed: If tm is the input graph and given the pair ( f , bc ) such that bc is (w0, w1, w2) , the correspondence with the weights in bc and the vertices of the face f is the following: w0 = source(halfedge(f,tm),tm) w1 = target(halfedge(f,tm),tm) w2 = target(next(halfedge(f,tm),tm),tm)
members.definition: typedef std::pair<face_descriptor, Barycentric_coordinates> CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::Face_location

members.name: Shortest_path_result
members.brief: The return type from shortest path distance queries.
members.detailed: Stores the distance to the nearest source point, and a Source_point_iterator to the source point itself.
members.definition: typedef std::pair<FT, Source_point_iterator> CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::Shortest_path_result

members.name: Surface_mesh_shortest_path
members.brief: creates a shortest paths object using tm as input.
members.detailed: Equivalent to Surface_mesh_shortest_path (tm, get(boost::vertex_index, tm), get(boost::halfedge_index, tm), get(boost::face_index, tm), get(CGAL::vertex_point, tm), traits) . Internal property maps must be available and initialized. CGAL::set_halfedgeds_items_id()
members.definition: CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::Surface_mesh_shortest_path

members.name: Surface_mesh_shortest_path
members.brief: creates a shortest paths object using tm as input.
members.detailed: No copy of the Triangle_mesh is made, only a reference to the tm is held. tm The surface mesh to compute shortest paths on. Note that it must be triangulated. vertexIndexMap Property map associating an id to each vertex, from 0 to num_vertices(tm) - 1 . halfedgeIndexMap Property map associating an id to each halfedge, from 0 to num_halfedges(tm) - 1 . faceIndexMap Property map associating an id to each face, from 0 to num_faces(tm) - 1 . vertexPointMap Property map used to access the points associated to each vertex of the graph. traits Optional instance of the traits class to use.
members.definition: CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::Surface_mesh_shortest_path
members.argsstring: (const Triangle_mesh &tm, Vertex_index_map vertexIndexMap, Halfedge_index_map halfedgeIndexMap, Face_index_map faceIndexMap, Vertex_point_map vertexPointMap, const Traits &traits=Traits())

members.name: add_source_point
members.brief: adds v as a source for the shortest path queries.
members.detailed: No change to the internal shortest paths data structure occurs until either Surface_mesh_shortest_path::build_sequence_tree() or the first shortest path query is done. An iterator to the source point added

members.detailed: No change to the internal shortest paths data structure occurs until either Surface_mesh_shortest_path::build_sequence_tree() or the first shortest path query is done. f A face of the input face graph location Barycentric coordinates in face f specifying the source point. An iterator to the source point added
members.definition: Source_point_iterator CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::add_source_point
members.section: Addition and Removal of Source Points

members.name: add_source_point
members.brief: adds a point inside a face as a source for the shortest path queries, equivalent to Surface_mesh_shortest_path::add_source_point (location.first, location.second);

members.section: Addition and Removal of Source Points

members.name: add_source_points
members.brief: adds a range of points as sources for the shortest path queries.
members.detailed: No change to the internal shortest paths data structure occurs until either Surface_mesh_shortest_path::build_sequence_tree() or the first shortest path query is done. InputIterator A ForwardIterator which dereferences to either Surface_mesh_shortest_path::Face_location , or Surface_mesh_shortest_path::vertex_descriptor . begin iterator to the first in the list of source point locations. end iterator to one past the end of the list of source point locations. An iterator to the first source point added.
members.definition: Source_point_iterator CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::add_source_points
members.section: Addition and Removal of Source Points

members.name: remove_source_point
members.brief: removes a source point for the shortest path queries.
members.detailed: No change to the internal shortest paths data structure occurs until either Surface_mesh_shortest_path::build_sequence_tree() or the first shortest path query is done. Behavior is undefined if the source point it was already removed. it iterator to the source point to be removed
members.definition: void CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::remove_source_point
members.section: Addition and Removal of Source Points

members.name: remove_all_source_points
members.brief: removes all source points for the shortest path queries.
members.detailed: No change to the internal shortest paths data structure occurs until either Surface_mesh_shortest_path::build_sequence_tree() or the first shortest path query is done. For a version which deletes all data immediately, use clear() instead.
members.definition: void CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::remove_all_source_points
members.section: Addition and Removal of Source Points

members.name: build_sequence_tree
members.brief: Computes all pending changes to the internal sequence tree.
members.detailed: A call to this method will only trigger a computation only if some change to the set of source points occurred since the last time the sequence tree was computed.
members.definition: void CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::build_sequence_tree
members.section: Creation and Destruction of the Shortest Paths Sequence Tree

members.name: clear
members.brief: removes all data, the class is as if it was constructed.
members.detailed: All internal containers are cleared and the internal sequence tree is also cleared. For a version which defers deletion until it is necessary, use Surface_mesh_shortest_path::remove_all_source_points() .
members.definition: void CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::clear
members.section: Creation and Destruction of the Shortest Paths Sequence Tree

members.name: source_points_begin
members.brief: returns an iterator to the first source point location
members.detailed: The elements will appear in the order they were inserted to the structure by calls to add_source_point() or add_source_points() . Deleted points will not appear in the sequence. An iterator to the first of the stored source points.
members.definition: Source_point_iterator CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::source_points_begin

members.name: source_points_end
members.brief: returns an iterator to one past the last source point location
members.detailed: An iterator to one past-the-end in the list of stored source points.
members.definition: Source_point_iterator CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::source_points_end

members.brief: returns the total number of source points used for the shortest path queries.
members.definition: std::size_t CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::number_of_source_points

members.name: changed_since_last_build
members.brief: determines if the internal sequence tree is valid (already built and no new source point has been added).
members.detailed: true if the structure needs to be rebuilt, false otherwise
members.definition: bool CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::changed_since_last_build

members.name: shortest_distance_to_source_points
members.brief: Computes the shortest surface distance from a vertex to any source point.
members.detailed: v A vertex of the input face graph A pair, containing the distance to the source point, and an iterator to the source point. If no source point was reachable (can occur when the graph is disconnected), the distance will be a negative value and the source point iterator will be equal to source_points_end() .
members.definition: Shortest_path_result CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::shortest_distance_to_source_points

members.name: shortest_distance_to_source_points
members.brief: Computes the shortest surface distance from any surface location to any source point.
members.detailed: f A face of the input face graph location Barycentric coordinates of the query point on face f A pair, containing the distance to the source point, and an iterator to the source point. If no source point was reachable (can occur when the graph is disconnected), the distance will be a negative value and the source point iterator will be equal to source_points_end() .
members.definition: Shortest_path_result CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::shortest_distance_to_source_points

members.name: shortest_path_sequence_to_source_points
members.brief: visits the sequence of edges, vertices and faces traversed by the shortest path from a vertex to any source point.
members.detailed: Visits simplices, starting from the query vertex, back to the nearest source point. If no shortest path could be found (for example, the surface is disconnected), then no calls to the visitor will be made (not even for the query vertex). v A vertex of the input face graph visitor A model of SurfaceMeshShortestPathVisitor to receive the shortest path A pair, containing the distance to the source point, and an iterator to the source point. If no source point was reachable (can occur when the graph is disconnected), the distance will be a negative value and the source point iterator will be equal to source_points_end() .
members.definition: Shortest_path_result CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::shortest_path_sequence_to_source_points

members.name: shortest_path_sequence_to_source_points
members.brief: visits the sequence of edges, vertices and faces traversed by the shortest path from any surface location to any source point.
members.detailed: Visits simplices, starting from the query point, back to the nearest source point. If no shortest path could be found (for example, the surface is disconnected), then no calls to the visitor will be made (not even for the query point). f A face of the input face graph location Barycentric coordinates of the query point on face f visitor A model of SurfaceMeshShortestPathVisitor to receive the shortest path A pair, containing the distance to the source point, and an iterator to the source point. If no source point was reachable (can occur when the graph is disconnected), the distance will be a negative value and the source point iterator will be equal to source_points_end() .
members.definition: Shortest_path_result CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::shortest_path_sequence_to_source_points

members.name: shortest_path_points_to_source_points
members.brief: Computes the sequence of points in the shortest path along the surface of the input face graph from the given vertex to the closest source point.
members.detailed: v A vertex of the input face graph output An OutputIterator to receive the shortest path points as Point_3 objects A pair, containing the distance to the source point, and an iterator to the source point. If no source point was reachable (can occur when the graph is disconnected), the distance will be a negative value and the source point iterator will be equal to source_points_end() .
members.definition: Shortest_path_result CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::shortest_path_points_to_source_points

members.name: shortest_path_points_to_source_points
members.brief: Computes the sequence of points in the shortest path along the surface of the input face graph from the given query location to the closest source point.
members.detailed: f A face of on the input face graph location The barycentric coordinates of the query point on face f output An OutputIterator to receive the shortest path points as Point_3 objects A pair, containing the distance to the source point, and an iterator to the source point. If no source point was reachable (can occur when the graph is disconnected), the distance will be a negative value and the source point iterator will be equal to source_points_end() .
members.definition: Shortest_path_result CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::shortest_path_points_to_source_points
members.argsstring: (const face_descriptor f, const Barycentric_coordinates &location, OutputIterator output)

members.name: locate
members.brief: returns the nearest face location to the given point.
members.detailed: Note that this will (re-)build an AABB_tree on each call. If you need to call this function more than once, use build_aabb_tree() to cache a copy of the AABB_tree , and use the overloads of this function that accept a reference to an AABB_tree as input. The following static overload is also available: static Face_location locate (const Point_3& p, const Triangle_mesh & tm, Vertex_point_map vertexPointMap, const Traits& traits = Traits()) AABBTraits A model of AABBTraits used to define a CGAL AABB_tree . p Point to locate on the input face graph
members.definition: Face_location CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::locate

members.name: locate
members.brief: returns the face location nearest to the given point.
members.detailed: The following static overload is also available: static Face_location locate(const Point_3& p, const AABB_tree<AABBTraits>& tree, const Triangle_mesh & tm, Vertex_point_map vertexPointMap, const Traits& traits = Traits()) AABBTraits A model of AABBTraits used to define a CGAL AABB_tree . p Point to locate on the input face graph tree A AABB_tree containing the triangular faces of the input surface mesh to perform the point location with
members.definition: Face_location CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::locate

members.name: locate
members.brief: returns the face location along ray nearest to its source point.
members.detailed: Note that this will (re-)build an AABB_tree on each call. If you need to call this function more than once, use build_aabb_tree() to cache a copy of the AABB_tree , and use the overloads of this function that accept a reference to an AABB_tree as input. The following static overload is also available: static Face_location locate (const Ray_3& ray, const Triangle_mesh & tm, Vertex_point_map vertexPointMap, const Traits& traits = Traits()) AABBTraits A model of AABBTraits used to define an AABB_tree . ray Ray to intersect with the input face graph
members.definition: Face_location CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::locate

members.definition: Face_location CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path< Traits, VIM, HIM, FIM, VPM >::locate

CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path::Source.name: CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path::Source_point_iterator
CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path::Source.brief: A model of BidirectionalIterator to access the source points.
CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path::Source.detailed: An iterator becomes invalid if: the corresponding point is removed (either with Surface_mesh_shortest_path::remove_source_point() or Surface_mesh_shortest_path::remove_all_source_points() ). the structure is re-built (triggered by a shortest path query or a call to Surface_mesh_shortest_path::build_sequence_tree() ). the structure is cleared ( Surface_mesh_shortest_path::clear() ). Dereferencing this iterator yields a const Surface_mesh_shortest_path::Face_location & . This iterator supports equality comparison operations.

SurfaceMeshShortestPathTraits.name: SurfaceMeshShortestPathTraits
SurfaceMeshShortestPathTraits.brief: The concept SurfaceMeshShortestPathTraits describes the types, predicates, and constructions required by the traits class parameter of CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path .
SurfaceMeshShortestPathTraits.detailed: Refines CopyConstructible Assignable Has models CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_path_traits <K,P>

members.name: Triangle_mesh
members.brief: A model of the concept FaceListGraph .

members.name: FT
members.brief: A model of the concept FieldWithSqrt or a model of both Field and RealEmbeddable .

members.name: Point_2
members.brief: The 2-dimensional point type.

members.name: Vector_2
members.brief: The 2-dimensional vector type.

members.name: Ray_2
members.brief: The 2-dimensional ray type.

members.name: Line_2
members.brief: The 2-dimensional line type.

members.name: Segment_2
members.brief: The 2-dimensional segment type.

members.name: Triangle_2
members.brief: The 2-dimensional triangle type.

members.name: Barycentric_coordinates
members.brief: An ordered triple to specify barycentric coordinates in triangles.

members.name: Point_3
members.brief: The 3-dimensional point type.

members.name: Vector_3
members.brief: The 3-dimensional vector type.

members.name: Ray_3
members.brief: The 3-dimensional ray type.

members.name: Triangle_3
members.brief: The 3-dimensional triangle type.

members.name: Construct_point_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides Point_2 operator()(FT x, FT y) and Point_2 operator()(CGAL::ORIGIN) to construct points with cartesian coordinates (x,y) and (0,0) respectively.

members.name: Construct_vector_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides Vector_2 operator()(Point_2 a, Point_2 b) to construct the vector b - a .

members.name: Construct_ray_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides Ray_2 operator()(Point_2 a, Point_2 b) to construct the ray originating from a and passing through b .

members.name: Construct_line_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides Line_2 operator()(Segment_2 s) and Line_2 operator()(Ray_2 r) to construct the supporting line to s and r respectively.

members.name: Construct_segment_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides Segment_2 operator()(Point_2 a, Point_2 b) to construct the segment (a,b) .

members.name: Construct_triangle_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides Triangle_2 operator()(Point_2 a, Point_2 b, Point_2 c) to construct the triangle (a,b,c) .

members.name: Construct_vertex_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides Point_2 operator()(Segment_2 s, int i) to return the source or target of s , depending on whether i is 0 or 1 respectively, and Point_2 operator()(Triangle_2 t, int i) to return the i -th vertex of t .

members.name: Construct_source_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides Point_2 operator()(Segment_2 s) to return the source of s .

members.name: Construct_target_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides Point_2 operator()(Segment_2 s) to return the target of s .

members.name: Construct_barycenter_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides Point_2 operator()(Point_2 p1, FT w1, Point_2 p2, FT w2) to compute the barycenter of the points p1 and p2 with corresponding weights w1 and w2 , and Point_2 operator()(Point_2 p1, FT w1, Point_2 p2, FT w2, Point_2 p3, FT w3) to compute the barycenter of the points p1 , p2 , and p3 with corresponding weights w1 , w2 , and w3 .

members.name: Compute_squared_distance_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides FT operator()(A obj1, B obj2) to compute the squared distance between obj1 and obj2 , A and B can be any one of Point_2 , Segment_2 , type objects.

members.name: Intersect_2
members.brief: Function object type.
members.detailed: Must provide std::optional< std::variant< T... > > operator()(A obj1, B obj2) to compute the intersection between obj1 and obj2 , where A and B can be any type amongst Line_2 , Ray_2 , Segment_2 .

members.name: Construct_point_on_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides Point_2 operator()(Ray_2 r, int i) to construct a point on r , such that i = 0 is the source, and any i > 0 is not the source.

members.name: Construct_vector_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides Vector_3 operator()(Point_3 a, Point_3 b) to construct the vector b - a .

members.name: Construct_triangle_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides Triangle_3 operator()(Point_3 a, Point_3 b, Point_3 c) to construct the triangle (a,b,c) .

members.name: Construct_vertex_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides Point_3 operator()(Segment_3 s, int i) to return the source or target of s , depending on whether i is 0 or 1 respectively, and Point_3 operator()(Triangle_3 t, int i) to return the i -th vertex of t .

members.name: Construct_source_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides Point_3 operator()(Segment_3 s) to return the source of s .

members.name: Construct_target_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides Point_3 operator()(Segment_3 s) to return the target of s .

members.name: Construct_barycenter_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides Point_3 operator()(Point_3 p1, FT w1, Point_3 p2) to compute the barycenter of the points p1 and p2 with corresponding weights w1 and 1-w1 , and Point_3 operator()(Point_3 p1, FT w1, Point_3 p2, FT w2, Point_3 p3, FT w3) to compute the barycenter of the points p1 , p2 , and p3 with corresponding weights w1 , w2 , and w3 .

members.name: Compute_squared_distance_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides FT operator()(Point_3 p1, Point_3 p2) to compute the squared distance between p1 and p2 .

members.name: Construct_triangle_3_to_triangle_2_projection
members.brief: Function object type that provides Triangle_2 operator()(Triangle_3 t) which computes a 2-dimensional projection of t that preserves edge lengths.

members.name: Construct_triangle_3_along_segment_2_flattening
members.brief: Function object type that provides Triangle_2 operator()(Triangle_3 t, std::size_t i, Segment_2 base) which computes a 2-dimensional projection of t that preserves edge lengths, such that the i -th edge of the projection of t is incident to base .
members.detailed: The length of the i -th edge of t is equal to the length of base

members.name: Compute_parametric_distance_along_segment_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides FT operator()(Point_2 x0, Point_2 x1, Point_2 p) which computes the parametric distance of p along the segment [x0,x1] .
members.detailed: That is, it computes t , such that p = (1.0 - t)*x0 + t*x1 p is a point in the segment [x0,x1]

members.name: Construct_barycentric_coordinates
members.brief: Function object type that provides Barycentric_coordinates operator()(FT a, FT b, FT c) to introduce new triangular barycentric coordinates.

members.name: Construct_barycentric_coordinates_weight
members.brief: Function object type that provides FT operator(Barycentric_coordinates b, std::size_t i) to get the i -th weight of barycentric coordinates b .

members.name: Construct_barycentric_coordinates_in_triangle_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides Barycentric_coordinates operator()(Triangle_2 t, Point_2 p) which computes the Barycentric location of p in t .

members.name: Construct_barycentric_coordinates_in_triangle_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides Barycentric_coordinates operator()(Triangle_3 t, Point_3 p) which computes the Barycentric location of p in t .

members.name: Classify_barycentric_coordinates
members.brief: Function object type that provides std::pair< CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_paths_3::Barycentric_coordinates_type ,std::size_t> operator()(Barycentric_coordinates b) , which computes the classification and the associated edge (if applicable) of the coordinates b .
members.detailed: Returns the pair ( type , i ), such that type is one of the values of CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_paths_3::Barycentric_coordinates_type If type is CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_paths_3::BARYCENTRIC_COORDINATES_ON_VERTEX , i is the index of that vertex If type is CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_paths_3::BARYCENTRIC_COORDINATES_ON_BOUNDARY , i is the index of the non-zero edge 0 if (0,1) are the non-zero coordinates 1 if (1,2) are the non-zero coordinates 2 if (2,0) are the non-zero coordinates Otherwise, the value of i is undefined.

members.name: Orientation_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides CGAL::Orientation operator()(Point_2 s1, Point_2 s2, Point_2 s3) , which performs an orientation test for the given points.

members.name: Compare_distance_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides CGAL::Comparison_result operator()(Point_2 p1, Point_2 p2, Point_2 p3, Point_2 p4) , which compares the squared distance between p1 and p2 with the squared distance between p3 and p4 .

members.name: Compare_relative_intersection_along_segment_2
members.brief: Function object type that provides CGAL::Comparison_result operator()(Segment_2 s1, Line_2 l1, Segment_2 s2, Line_2 l2) .
members.detailed: This compares the relative parametric intersections of s1 with l1 against s2 with l2 . That is, compare the distance of the intersection of s1 with l1 from the source of s1 , scaled by the length of s1 , to the distance of the intersection of s2 with l2 from the source of s2 , scaled by the length of s2 . the intersection of s1 and l1 is a point the intersection of s2 and l2 is a point

members.name: Is_saddle_vertex
members.brief: Function object type that provides template <class VertexPointMap> bool operator()(boost::graph_traits< Triangle_mesh >::vertex_descriptor v, Triangle_mesh & tm, VertexPointMap vpm) that returns true if the vertex is a saddle vertex (more than $ 2 \pi $ surface area over all adjacent faces), and false otherwise.
members.detailed: vpm must be a model of concept ReadablePropertyMap that maps from vertex_descriptor to Point_3 objects.

members.name: construct_point_2_object
members.brief: returns the point construction functor.

SurfaceMeshShortestPathVisitor.name: SurfaceMeshShortestPathVisitor
SurfaceMeshShortestPathVisitor.brief: The concept SurfaceMeshShortestPathVisitor describes the requirements of the visitor type used to collect the edges and vertices traversed by a shortest path on the surface of a triangulated surface mesh.
SurfaceMeshShortestPathVisitor.detailed: The methods are called in the order of the shortest path sequence along the surface, starting with the target point and ending with the source point.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Called when an edge was traversed in the shortest path sequence.
members.detailed: edge halfedge of the surface mesh crossed by the shortest path. The halfedge is directed toward the face nearest to the target point. alpha value in the range [0.0,1.0] specifying where along edge the shortest path crossed. 0.0 means it crossed at source(edge) 1.0 means it crossed at target(edge) Any other value is linearly interpolated between the endpoints. Note that values of 0.0 and 1.0 are possible in some situations, and may not be reported as vertices. In particular, this will occur if the vertex crossed by the path is not a saddle vertex (less than $2\pi$ surface area). This is because from the perspective of the algorithm, this is indistinguishable from crossing anywhere else along the edge.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Called when a vertex is encountered in the shortest path sequence.
members.detailed: vertex the vertex of the surface mesh encountered by the shortest path.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Called when a face location is encountered in the shortest path sequence.
members.detailed: This will only be called as the first and/or last element in the path sequence, and only if the target/source point is an internal face location (i.e. not on an edge or at a vertex). face a face of the surface mesh encountered at the start or the end of the shortest path. location the barycentric coordinates inside face of this point on the path.

CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_paths_3.name: CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_paths_3
CGAL::Surface_mesh_shortest_paths_3.brief: Namespace containing support members specific to this package.

members.name: Barycentric_coordinates_type
members.brief: Classification of the location of a 3-tuple barycentric coordinates in a triangle.

PkgSurfaceMeshShortestPathRef.name: PkgSurfaceMeshShortestPathRef
PkgSurfaceMeshShortestPathRef.detailed: Todo Modify the algorithm to support more efficient incremental construction Add parallelization to the algorithm Add methods for computing the ridge tree using the output of the algorithm Add methods for computing shortest paths from geodesic sources as well Stephen Kiazyk, Sébastien Loriot, and Éric Colin de Verdière The package provides methods for computing geodesic shortest path on triangulated surface meshes. The algorithm used is based on a paper by Xin and Wang [3] . The input of this package can be any model of the FaceListGraph concept.
